section1.paragraph2: Access to the website implies full and unreserved acceptance of these conditions by the user.

section2.title: 2. Use of the website

section3.title: 3. Website content
section3.paragraph1: provides information about towns, culture, traditions, and events for informational purposes.
section3.paragraph2: Although every effort is made to keep the information updated and accurate, the absence of errors or changes in the events or activities described cannot be guaranteed.

section5.title: 5. Modifications
section5.paragraph1: reserves the right to modify these conditions at any time in order to adapt them to legal changes or to the operation of the website.

section6.title: 6. Applicable law

section1.title: 1. Identification details
section1.paragraph1: In compliance with the information duty established in Law 34/2002 on Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce (LSSI-CE), users of this website are informed of the following details:
section1.activityValue: Digital platform for cultural, tourism, and event information.
section1.paragraph2: Access to and use of the website grants the status of user and implies full acceptance of the conditions established in this Legal Notice.

section2.title: 2. Purpose of the website

paragraph1.after: aims to provide information about towns, traditions, festivities, culture, and events, mainly in the Community of Madrid, in order to promote cultural knowledge and local tourism.

section2.paragraph2: The published content is for informational purposes and may be updated, modified, or removed without prior notice.

section3.title: 3. Conditions of use
section3.paragraph1: The user agrees to make appropriate use of the website and its contents, respecting current legislation, good faith, and public order.
section3.prohibited1: Using the website for unlawful purposes or purposes contrary to current regulations.
section3.prohibited2: Causing damage to the computer systems of the website or third parties.
section3.prohibited3: Attempting to access restricted areas without authorization.
section3.prohibited4: Using the site's content for commercial purposes without express authorization.

section4.title: 4. Intellectual and industrial property
section4.paragraph1: All website content, including texts, images, logos, design, structure, source code, and other elements, is protected by intellectual and industrial property rights.
section4.paragraph2: The reproduction, distribution, or public communication of the content without the express authorization of the website owner is prohibited.

section5.title: 5. Liability
section5.paragraph1: The website owner is not responsible for:
section5.item1: Possible errors or omissions in the content.
section5.item3: Damage resulting from the use of the published information.
section5.paragraph2: Likewise, the website may include links to external pages over which it has no control or responsibility.

section6.title: 6. Applicable law
section6.paragraph1: The relationship between the user and the website owner shall be governed by current Spanish law, and the corresponding courts and tribunals shall have jurisdiction in accordance with the applicable regulations.

questions.question: How can I add a new event?
questions.answer: Click on "Add event" on the main calendar page. Complete the form with the event details and submit it for review.

questions.question: Why does my event not appear immediately?
questions.answer: All submitted events must be approved by a moderator. Once approved, they will appear on the calendar.

questions.question: How can I see the details of an event?
questions.answer: Click on a day in the calendar. The events scheduled for that day will appear and you will be able to view their details.

questions.question: Can I edit or delete an event I created?
questions.answer: At the moment, it is not possible to edit or delete events from the interface. Contact support if you need to make changes.

questions.question: Do I need to be registered to view events?
questions.answer: No, you can view events without registering. However, you need an account to add events or comment.

questions.question: How do I register?
questions.answer: Click on "Create account" at the top of the site and complete the registration form.

questions.question: What happens if I forget my password?
questions.answer: On the login page, click on "Forgot your password?" and follow the instructions to reset it.

questions.question: How can I leave a comment on an event?
questions.answer: Click on the event and then on "Comment on this event". Select a rating and write your opinion.

questions.question: Can I edit or delete my comment?
questions.answer: It is currently not possible to edit or delete comments once they have been submitted.

questions.question: Why can't I comment on some events?
questions.answer: Only registered users can comment. Also, you cannot comment on events that you created yourself.

questions.question: The calendar does not load, what should I do?
questions.answer: Make sure you have an internet connection. If the problem persists, try reloading the page or clearing your browser cache.

questions.question: The event form is not being submitted, what could be wrong?
questions.answer: Check that all required fields are completed and that you are logged in. If the error persists, contact support.

questions.question: What data of mine is public when creating an event?
questions.answer: Only your name and profile picture are shown. Your email address and other personal data are not publicly visible.

questions.question: How can I delete my account?
questions.answer: To delete your account, contact our support team indicating your username and registered email.

questions.question: Can my comments be seen by other users?
questions.answer: Yes, comments are public and visible to anyone who accesses the event.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Do you have an event, an experience, or an idea that deserves to be discovered?

text1.before: Do you want to collaborate with
text1.after: or do you simply have a quick question? This is the right place. Write to us and we will reply quickly, clearly, and without any fuss.

contact.text2: Promise: we read every message (and real people reply, not bots). Fill out the form and tell us how we can help. The more context you give us, the better we can guide you.
topics: Collaborations with town councils or creators

section1.title: 1. What cookies are
section1.paragraph1: Cookies are small text files that are stored on the user's device when visiting a website. Their purpose is to improve the browsing experience, analyze site usage, and offer content adapted to the user's interests.

section2.title: 2. Types of cookies used
section2.paragraph1: This website may use the following types of cookies:

technical.title: Technical cookies
technical.text: They allow the basic functioning of the website and access to certain features.

analytics.title: Analytics cookies
analytics.text: They allow the analysis of user behavior on the website in order to improve the browsing experience and optimize content.

customization.title: Customization cookies
customization.text: They allow remembering user preferences, such as language or browsing settings.

thirdParty.title: Third-party cookies
thirdParty.text: In some cases, third-party services may be used to analyze website usage or improve certain services.

section3.title: 3. Cookie management
section3.paragraph1: The user may allow, block, or delete the cookies installed on their device through the settings of the browser they use.
section3.paragraph2: Each browser provides specific options for managing cookies.

section4.title: 4. Acceptance of the cookies policy
section4.paragraph1: When accessing the website, the user will see an informational notice about the use of cookies. By continuing to browse, it is considered that the user accepts their use under the conditions described in this policy.

section5.title: 5. Updating the cookies policy
section5.paragraph1: The person responsible for the website may modify this cookies policy according to legislative or technical changes. It is recommended to review this section periodically.

section1.title: 1. Information for the user
section1.paragraph1: In compliance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR) and Organic Law 3/2018 on Personal Data Protection, we inform users about the processing of their personal data.

paragraph2.before: The person responsible for processing the data collected through this website is

section2.title: 2. Personal data collected
section2.paragraph1: Through this website, data may be collected through:

section3.title: 3. Purpose of data processing
section3.item3: Improve the operation of the website

section4.title: 4. Data retention
section4.paragraph1: Data will only be kept for the time necessary to fulfill the purpose for which it was collected.

section5.title: 5. User rights
section5.paragraph1: The user may exercise the rights of access, rectification, deletion, objection, restriction, and portability of their data.

section6.title: 6. Data security
section6.paragraph1: The person responsible adopts the necessary technical measures to protect personal data from unauthorized access.

top.subtitle: PLANS WITH HISTORY
top.description: Cultural, gastronomic, and nature experiences to enjoy your surroundings like never before. Book in minutes and save your favorites.

bottom.title: FEATURED EXPERIENCES THIS MONTH
bottom.text1: Every month we select unique plans so you can discover your surroundings from a different perspective: history, gastronomy, nature, and traditions worth telling.
bottom.text2: Check the calendar and book your next experience

about.title: Who We Are

paragraph1.after: we believe that the best plans are not always found in guides, big cities, or in algorithms that decide for us.

about.paragraph2: Often, they are much closer: in a village, in a neighborhood, in a square where someone has decided to keep a tradition alive.

paragraph3.after: was born with a clear idea: to give visibility to those local events that are part of our culture, but often go unnoticed. Popular festivals, local fairs, pilgrimages, traditional celebrations that, without visibility, risk being lost over time.

about.paragraph4: We are a collaborative platform that connects people, places, and traditions. A space where any user can discover authentic experiences or share those worth living. Every published event goes through a review process, because we believe that quality and respect for local culture are essential.
about.paragraph5: Our goal goes beyond informing: we seek to promote more conscious, decentralized, and human tourism that supports small towns and helps keep their customs alive.
about.highlight: is not just an events agenda.
about.paragraph6: It is a community that believes in authenticity, closeness, and the value of what is not always visible.
about.cta: Because sometimes, the best plan… is the one you still don't know.

support.title: Technical support
support.subtitle: If something is not working as it should, we are here to help.

text1.after: we work so that technology is a tool, not a problem. This space is designed to solve technical issues quickly, simply, and effectively, so you can continue enjoying the platform without interruptions.

support.text2: ⚙️ What kind of issues do we solve? Problems accessing your account, errors in bookings or payments, difficulties accessing courses or content, loading or display issues, and problems when publishing or managing events.
